Title: Alternaria alternata Effector Aa593 Promotes Virulence by Hijacking the CmNAC29-Mediated Abscisic Acid Biosynthesis Pathway in Chrysanthemum

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Novel secreted effector protein with N-terminal signal peptide (aa 1–17); no transmembrane domain, no GPI-anchor, no conserved domain reported
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plant cell nucleus; also distributed throughout tobacco cells upon transient expression; nucleus in fungal/plant infection observations
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hormone involved in plant growth/development and stress signaling; in this pathosystem promotes susceptibility to Alternaria alternata
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA accumulation indirectly by enhancing CmNAC29-mediated activation of CmNCED3, thereby increasing chrysanthemum susceptibility and fungal virulence
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for plant overexpression; native Aa593 promoter used for secretion/localization assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Alternaria alternata infection; exogenous ABA pretreatment (100 μM); fluridone ABA-biosynthesis inhibitor in Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Aa593 is a novel A. alternata effector that physically interacts with chrysanthemum CmNAC29 in the plant nucleus. 2. Aa593 enhances the transcriptional activation activity of CmNAC29 on CmNCED3 without changing CmNAC29 DNA-binding ability. 3. Aa593 overexpression elevates CmNCED3 expression and ABA content in chrysanthemum, while ΔAa593 mutants show reduced virulence that can be partly compensated by exogenous ABA.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in OE-Aa593 chrysanthemum lines; Aa593-mediated effects assessed in infected leaves/protoplasts
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; LCI; BiFC; Co-IP; GST pull-down; fungal gene deletion and complementation; plant overexpression; dual-luciferase/protoplast transient assays; ABA quantification; pathogenicity assays
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly upregulated at 6 hpi during infection
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for disease-resistance engineering by disrupting Aa593 delivery or its interaction with CmNAC29; useful for screening resistant germplasm insensitive to effector-enhanced ABA susceptibility
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Aa593 also affects fungal hyphal growth; blocking its action may alter pathogen fitness but no plant tradeoff established
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Aa593 has transcriptional self-activation activity in yeast, does not directly bind the CmNCED3 promoter, and virulence depends partly on the ABA pathway; stable Aa593 overexpression in Arabidopsis and transient expression in N. benthamiana also enhanced susceptibility

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Abscisic acid (ABA) biosynthesis from carotenoid precursors
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed/rate-limiting upstream step in ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established as a key rate-limiting enzyme gene for ABA synthesis in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes susceptibility to A. alternata in chrysanthemum in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher CmNCED3 expression increases ABA biosynthesis and susceptibility; silencing CmNCED3 enhances resistance to A. alternata
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: A. alternata infection; exogenous ABA pretreatment (100 μM)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CmNCED3 was the most prominently downregulated ABA-pathway gene in amiR-CmNAC29 plants. 2. CmNAC29 directly binds the CmNCED3 promoter at a CACG element and activates transcription. 3. VIGS-mediated silencing of CmNCED3 reduces disease lesion area, linking CmNCED3-dependent ABA biosynthesis to susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in leaves; induced to higher levels in WT and OE-CmNAC29/OE-Aa593 relative to interference or control lines, especially under infection
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native CmNCED3 promoter used in Y1H, ChIP-qPCR, dual-luciferase and protoplast reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase reporter assay; VIGS/amiR silencing; ABA quantification; pathogen inoculation phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for reducing ABA-mediated disease susceptibility through promoter editing of CmNAC29-binding site or downregulation in leaves under infection
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ABA is needed for normal growth and development; reducing CmNCED3 may compromise ABA-dependent physiological processes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper states CmNCED3 is the dominant NCED isoform mediating ABA biosynthesis/stress responses in this system

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC family transcription factor with N-terminal NAM domain (aa 9–134)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CmNCED3 transcription by binding a CACG cis-element in its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of ABA accumulation and a susceptibility factor that determines the extent of A. alternata infection in chrysanthemum
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: A. alternata infection; Aa593 effector interaction enhances its transactivation activity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with Aa593 effector in the plant nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hormone contributing to susceptibility to necrotrophic fungus A. alternata in chrysanthemum
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA biosynthesis via direct activation of CmNCED3, thereby increasing susceptibility to A. alternata
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: A. alternata infection; exogenous ABA pretreatment (100 μM)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in roots and leaves; continuously induced during A. alternata infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in roots and leaves; continuously induced during A. alternata infection
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 2×35S promoter for amiR-CmNAC29 interference; 35S promoter for overexpression and transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: amiR interference; overexpression; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase reporter assay; ABA measurement; pathogen inoculation; protoplast assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising susceptibility-gene target for editing or knockdown to reduce ABA-mediated disease susceptibility; could combine with disruption of Aa593 recognition/interaction surfaces
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: CmNAC29 may support basal ABA needed for growth/development, so constitutive suppression could affect normal physiology
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike Arabidopsis ANAC029/NAP reported to target AAO3, chrysanthemum CmNAC29 regulates ABA biosynthesis mainly through CmNCED3 in this study
